title: Post-tonsillectomy follow-up
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports recording clinician-completed postoperative education, pain and hydration context, bleeding outcomes, monitoring, and follow-up. Medicines, doses, admission, discharge, and treatment instructions are excluded.
assessment.rationale: The exact paediatric postoperative section supports interval change, pain and functional impact, hydration or bleeding context through associated symptoms and relevant negatives, vital signs, general appearance, ENT examination, clinician concern, entered plan, safety net, and follow-up without giving medication, dose, admission, discharge, or treatment instructions.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Tonsillectomy in Children (Update)
source.jurisdiction: United States specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE paediatric ENT, anaesthesia, analgesia, and postoperative adaptation
source.population: Children aged 1 to 18 years being considered for or followed after tonsillectomy.
source.setting: Any clinician setting involved in paediatric tonsillectomy assessment, perioperative education, and postoperative outcome review.
source.exclusions: Exact for recurrent throat-infection documentation, modifying factors, sleep-related comorbidity context, perioperative education, postoperative pain or bleeding review, and outcome assessment. It does not generate surgery, analgesia, antibiotic, steroid, dose, admission, or discharge instructions.

exact_location.heading: Key action statements 9, 12, and 15 — pain education, monitoring context, and bleeding outcomes
exact_location.locator: official guideline key action statements 9, 12, and 15
